sections.text:
(1) Subject to the provisions of this Act, a mobile money service provider shall collect a levy set out in the Schedule on a person to person transfer .
Subject to the provisions of this Act, a mobile money service provider shall collect a levy set out in the Schedule on a person to person transfer .
(2) The levy referred to under subsection (1) shall be paid by the sender of the electronic money.
The levy referred to under subsection (1) shall be paid by the sender of the electronic money.
(3) A mobile money service provider shall, within ten days after the end of each month, submit a return of the levy collected to the Commissioner-General in the manner determined by the Commissioner-General .
A mobile money service provider shall, within ten days after the end of each month, submit a return of the levy collected to the Commissioner-General in the manner determined by the Commissioner-General .
(4) A mobile money service provider shall, within ten days after the end of each month, remit the total levies collected for that month to the Commissioner-General .
A mobile money service provider shall, within ten days after the end of each month, remit the total levies collected for that month to the Commissioner-General .
(5) A mobile money service provider that contravenes subsections (3) or (4) is liable to pay to the Commissioner-General a penalty of one hundred thousand penalty units for each month or part thereof during which that contravention continues.
A mobile money service provider that contravenes subsections (3) or (4) is liable to pay to the Commissioner-General a penalty of one hundred thousand penalty units for each month or part thereof during which that contravention continues.
(6) The Commissioner-General may waive the whole, or part, of the penalty due under subsection (5) of this section.
The Commissioner-General may waive the whole, or part, of the penalty due under subsection (5) of this section.

(1) A mobile money service provider shall keep and maintain a record of the mobile money service provider ’s operations for a period of six years.
A mobile money service provider shall keep and maintain a record of the mobile money service provider ’s operations for a period of six years.
(2) Despite subsection (1) , the Commissioner-General may, before the expiry of the period referred to under subsection (1) , by notice in writing, extend the period of keeping and maintaining a record of operations by a mobile money service provider.
Despite subsection (1) , the Commissioner-General may, before the expiry of the period referred to under subsection (1) , by notice in writing, extend the period of keeping and maintaining a record of operations by a mobile money service provider.
(3) A mobile money service provider that contravenes subsection (1) is liable to pay the Commissioner-General a penalty of two hundred thousand penalty units.
A mobile money service provider that contravenes subsection (1) is liable to pay the Commissioner-General a penalty of two hundred thousand penalty units.
(4) The Commissioner-General may waive the whole, or part, of the penalty due under subsection (3) .
The Commissioner-General may waive the whole, or part, of the penalty due under subsection (3) .

(1) The Commissioner-General may, for the purposes of enforcing the provisions of this Act, at any reasonable time, without prior notice, enter any premises that the Commissioner-General has reasonable grounds to believe is used contrary to the provisions of this Act, and— (a) inspect any records, article or document of a mobile money service provider that has a bearing on an inspection; (b) verify the returns submitted in accordance with section 5 (3) ; or (c) require the mobile money service provider to submit any other information that the Commissioner-General may consider necessary.
inspect any records, article or document of a mobile money service provider that has a bearing on an inspection;
verify the returns submitted in accordance with section 5 (3) ; or
require the mobile money service provider to submit any other information that the Commissioner-General may consider necessary.

(1) The Mobile Money Transaction Levy Act, 2023 is repealed.
The Mobile Money Transaction Levy Act, 2023 is repealed.
(2) On commencement of this Act,— (a) a mobile money service provider shall remit the total levies collected for each month to the Zambia Revenue Authority ; and (b) the Bank of Zambia shall transmit to the Zambia Revenue Authority any records, returns or documents relating to a levy remitted by a mobile money service provider under the repealed Act .
a mobile money service provider shall remit the total levies collected for each month to the Zambia Revenue Authority ; and
the Bank of Zambia shall transmit to the Zambia Revenue Authority any records, returns or documents relating to a levy remitted by a mobile money service provider under the repealed Act .
